headline: Oklahoma Holds Off Notre Dame Comeback in Offensive Shootout
body: Oklahoma's defense made the plays that mattered most, holding Notre Dame to a field goal in the final quarter despite CJ Carr's surgical performance through the air. Carr finished 20 of 25 for 270 yards and three touchdowns, moving the Notre Dame offense with precision and timing that kept his team within striking distance all afternoon. Yet it was Oklahoma's ability to answer—Glass orchestrating his own three-touchdown afternoon while the Oklahoma rushing attack found 39 more yards on the ground than its counterpart—that ultimately proved too much to overcome in Norman.

The numbers tell a tight story. Oklahoma outgained Notre Dame by just 25 yards and actually threw for fewer yards, but the Sooners converted opportunities into points where it counted. Glass completed 21 of 33 passes for 276 yards, and while his completion percentage trailed Carr's, he managed the game's critical moments. Notre Dame's Southall gave the Irish fits with 123 receiving yards and a score, a dominant performance on one side of the ball that still wasn't enough when the rest of the offense stalled in crucial spots.

Clean football from both sides—no turnovers—meant field position and execution determined the outcome. Oklahoma's Bernard carried six times for 73 yards, and while those numbers don't jump off the page, they came at moments when Notre Dame needed a stop and didn't get one. The Irish had chances to seize this game but couldn't quite sustain the drive sequences their talent suggested they should.
turningPoint: With the game tied late, Oklahoma's defense forced Notre Dame into a three-and-out while Glass led a methodical final drive that ended in a go-ahead score, preventing the Irish from ever taking the lead in the fourth quarter.

unsungHero.name: McNeil
unsungHero.why: Five catches for 107 yards provided Oklahoma's pass catchers with consistent separation and a safety valve beneath coverage, absorbing targets without forcing Glass to take risks downfield while Carr's more explosive receivers got the headline attention.
